title: Dynamic Glazing Tint Levels
summary: Dynamic architectural glazing typically corresponds to four visible light transmittance levels: 4%, 20%, 40%, and 60%.
evidence_quote: Typically, the tint corresponds to 4%, 20%, 40%, and 60% visible light transmittance Tvis depending on manufacturers.

title: Dynamic Glazing Application Feasibility
summary: Dynamic glazing is most feasible on large glazing facades not facing north, working well on east and west orientations.
evidence_quote: Dynamic glazing is usually the most feasible on large glazing facades not do not face north. It works well on east and west orientations since these are difficult to horizontally shade.

title: Dynamic Glazing Control Method
summary: Dynamic glazing is controlled by a low voltage current and can be operated manually or via an automatic building management system.
evidence_quote: The dynamic glazing is usually controlled by a low voltage current and can be controlled either manually through a switch or linked into an automatic building management system (BMS).

title: Desiccant Dehumidification Application Threshold
summary: Applications requiring a dew point at or below 40°F (4.3°C) may be candidates for active desiccant dehumidification.
evidence_quote: In general, applications that require a dew point at or below 40°F (4.3°C) may be candidates for active desiccant dehumidification.

title: Desiccant Dehumidifier Process Air Temperature Rise
summary: The process air outlet temperature rises roughly in proportion to the amount of moisture removed due to the heat of sorption.
evidence_quote: The outlet temperature rises roughly in proportion to the amount of moisture that is removed.

title: Desiccant Dehumidifier Reactivation Temperature Effect
summary: The reactivation air inlet temperature changes the outlet moisture content of the process air.
evidence_quote: The reactivation air inlet temperature changes the outlet moisture content of the process air.

title: Solar Thermal Application Range
summary: Solar thermal applications range from low-temperature (e.g., pool heating) to medium- or high-temperature (e.g., space heating, absorption cooling).
evidence_quote: Solar energy thermal applications range from low-temperature applications (swimming pool heating or domestic hot water) up to medium- or high-temperature applications (space heating, absorption cooling or steam production for electrical generation).

title: Solar Combi System Collector Area Requirement
summary: Solar combi systems for combined DHW and space heating require a larger collector area than a DHW system alone.
evidence_quote: Combi systems require a larger collector area than a DHW system to meet the higher loads.
